rationale.R1.2: A general request to look at a research paragraph centrally includes checking whether the argument from survey scale to ensemble disagreement and design implications is coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The paragraph makes quantitative performance and novelty-flag claims, so checking whether the stated evidence supports those claims is an expected responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation targets a paragraph, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision are load-bearing.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of a broad review request must disclose uncertainty about the intended depth of review.
rationale.RX.2: The review responsibility includes avoiding endorsement of claims that exceed what the paragraph reports.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within the paragraph-review scope and not perform the underlying research or rewrite the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The agent should avoid recommendations or judgments that could mislead downstream scientific interpretation.
rationale.RX.5: The agent should ground any responsibility in traceable features of the provided paragraph.
